title: Verify report (machine-checkable extract)
description: The units/<UID>/verify.json artifact (JSON-only; no verify.md). Encodes the executor<->verifier LOOP contract + the verifier-independence invariant + the premise-check (the independent COUNTER re-run). verdict/iteration/feedback/defects/socratic/premise_check are required. executor_reasoning_seen MUST be false (independence). FAIL is schema-INVALID unless it carries >=1 defect (each naming a criterion) AND feedback.actionable_changes is non-empty; PASS (I6 clause REVISED for coverage-first, PR1) requires NO blocker/major defect but MAY carry `minor` observations; DISAGREE requires a disagreement object. Optional PR1 fields: `panel[]` (odd-panel-of-3 with distinct correctness/reproduce/guardrail lenses; top verdict == discrete majority, no softmax), `verify_rounds`/`converged` (bounded loop-until-dry). The defect.criterion in brief.acceptance_criteria membership + the I16 panel discipline are cross-checked in validate_run.py.

executor_reasoning_seen.description: Independence invariant: the verifier saw brief+debrief+artifacts only, never the executor's chain-of-thought. MUST be false (maker!=checker). Self-attestation, not a platform guarantee (see state-machine Limitation A).

audit_notes.description: Optional free-form record of the verifier's adversarial work (per-criterion independent check, evidence audit, hallucination sweep, budget check) that formerly lived in verify.md prose. Not required — the load-bearing outputs are verdict/feedback/defects/premise_check.

panel.description: OPTIONAL. When a unit is verified by an odd panel (>=3) of INDEPENDENT verifiers with DISTINCT lenses (PR1 verifier hardening), record each panelist here. The top-level `verdict` MUST equal the DISCRETE majority of the panel verdicts (no softmax); a split with no strict majority MUST route to DISAGREE (AO-5). Members must cover the canonical lens trio correctness/reproduce/guardrail (not 3 clones). REQUIRED by validate_run.py I16 on any unit tagged `high-stakes`. Node-internal: recording a panel adds no FSM edge (termination proof untouched).

lens.description: The panelist's distinct lens: correctness (criteria + evidence), reproduce (re-run/re-derive the result), guardrail (scope/non-goal/gold-plating compliance).

verify_rounds.description: OPTIONAL. Number of loop-until-dry adversarial sweeps run INSIDE this single VERIFY node (defects accumulate across rounds until a round surfaces no NEW defect — 'dry' — or the R_max=3 cap is hit). Node-internal work that adds NO FSM edge; validate_run.py (I16) checks 1<=verify_rounds<=3 (finiteness). See references/self-learning-loops.md §2/§3.

converged.description: OPTIONAL. true iff the loop-until-dry sweep stopped because a round surfaced no NEW defect ('dry'); false iff it stopped at the R_max round cap (coverage may be incomplete — surface honestly).

feedback.description: Feedback object. actionable_changes = concrete edits for the next iteration (must be non-empty on FAIL); do_not_touch = what was correct and must be preserved.

defects.description: Top-level defect list. PASS => no blocker/major defect (minor observations allowed — I6 revised for coverage-first, PR1); FAIL => >=1, each citing a brief acceptance criterion (membership cross-checked by the validator).

socratic.description: The VERIFIER's own CANONICAL 4-key socratic block on its verdict. Same shape + rules as the debrief block.

premise_check.description: Backstop against premise deflection (the independent COUNTER re-run): the verifier attests it first confirmed the executor's stated premise is the deliverable's LOAD-BEARING claim, then RE-RAN COUNTER on it independently (decoupled, from evidence, never by reading executor reasoning). Provides the field; the templates wire the prose.

disagreement.description: Present iff verdict==DISAGREE: why evidence cannot settle it (routes to Phase 7). The iff is now enforced BOTH directions (N-06/PR-6): the DISAGREE=>present clause plus the non-DISAGREE=>absent clause in allOf below.

guardrail_compliance.description: OPTIONAL (guardrail enforcement). Per-verify attestation rows, one per non-goal checked: `non_goal` MUST be a VERBATIM clarifications.json non_goals string. Adoption-closure (validate_run.py I22, offline post-hoc): once ANY verdict-bearing verify carries the block, EVERY verdict-bearing verify must; membership + coverage of the unit's non_goal_refs are mechanical; one decidable semantic clause — a `violated` row on a PASS verdict is a FAIL (a delivered non-goal is a FAIL, not a bonus). Whether a `respected` row is TRUE stays verifier attestation (presence-not-genuineness).

allOf.description: disagreement present IFF verdict==DISAGREE — the => direction (N-06/PR-6): a non-DISAGREE verdict (PASS or FAIL) MUST NOT carry a disagreement object. The <= direction (DISAGREE=>present) is the dedicated clause further below. Uses `not` (built-in mini-validator extended for this — jsonschema supports it natively), so both backends agree.
FAIL => >=1 defect AND non-empty feedback.actionable_changes (evidence-bound critique).

PASS => no blocker/major defect (I6 PASS-clause, REVISED for coverage-first, PR1). Was `defects==[]`; now a PASS MAY carry `minor` observations (report every finding + severity, filter downstream) but MUST NOT carry a blocker/major defect — a blocking defect is incompatible with acceptance. A PASS with defects==[] still validates (backward-compatible). See references/self-learning-loops.md §5 (I6 revision + do_not_touch/AO-2 interplay) and references/state-machine.md I6.
DISAGREE => a disagreement object is present AND complete (why_unresolvable set) (routes to the Phase-7 gate).
